parentQuestion: Striking the "funny bone" (ulnar nerve) may cause injury to a nerve of which ple...

scaffolds.q: The brachial plexus supplies nerves to the arm and hand.
scaffolds.explain: The brachial plexus (C5-T1) is the network of nerves that supplies motor and sensory innervation to the ENTIRE upper limb — shoulder, arm, forearm, and hand. Every movement, every sensation, originates from this cable distribution system in your neck. Wonderfully designed. I want you to see the full scope of this system...

scaffolds.q: The ulnar nerve controls muscles in the hand.
scaffolds.explain: The ulnar nerve controls most of the small intrinsic muscles of the hand — the ones that spread and close your fingers, stabilize your grip, and let you perform fine motor tasks. Damage the ulnar nerve, and your hand loses precision and power. Allow this to wash over you:drawn into this delicate machinery — such complexity, such fragility...
